variant: Bandbox — Unified Design System (v1.0). "The Survey Table, Warmed." Brutalist bones, civic-warm air: Rowhouse's transparency mechanics + warmth rebuilt entirely in BRAND.md's hard-bordered hardware, with true Phillies Red kept to a 1-2-per-screen signal and blue doing all the structural and data lifting.
concept: From Variant A (Survey Print) I take the brutalist-FORWARD structural mass: the promoted 4px party-wall frames on primary regions, the three-tier offset-shadow system (6/8/10px) with the heaviest reserved for the single page-defining block, the visible 12-col grid with 3px column rules, and — critically — A's concrete, accountable red BUDGET MATH ("distress hero = 1, primary CTA = 2, budget spent, everything else drops to ink/ghost"), which is the most enforceable version of red discipline. A's map "instrument readout" (cursor coords + scale bar on the blueprint ground) and its surveyor corner-registration ticks on the active parcel are also adopted — they make the map read as a survey table, not a web map. From Variant B (Warmth-Balanced) I take everything that keeps the instrument humane without softening one brutalist constant: the named warm-air materials (--pb-paper-warm gutter behind card groups, --pb-rail as the warmest near-white teach surface), B's loosened inter-block rhythm (34px group gutters + a new 48px section step) so blocks breathe, the warm-UMBER dark ground (the brief mandates "warm-dark, not blue-black," so B's umber wins over A's bluer #161B22 while keeping A's discipline of moving the whole UI onto the blueprint family), and B's rule that the Zodiak voice earns more square footage (generous measure on explainers/teach rail) so warmth is carried by the serif's room, not by rounding or blur. Where the two forked I resolved toward the brief: dark = warm-umber blueprint (B's hue, A's "everything-on-blueprint" rigor); the Livability warm lens reconciles A's aged-amber and B's terracotta into one matched-lightness #B5703A; brick gets B's slightly-wider editorial role (drop-cap, section rule, history-tag border) but stays strictly text/edge-only per A's hard separation. Net: A is the skeleton and the discipline, B is the air and the voice.
lightTokens:
Concrete + patriotic-blue world (Mortar ground, Bone surfaces), red as signal only. Hex is authoritative; oklch is for tooling/interpolation. Extends repo TOKENS.css names so the build doesn't churn.
STRUCTURE/TEXT: --pb-ink (3px borders + primary text, warm near-black) #1A1714 ~oklch(.205 .006 60); --pb-slate (muted text/labels/metadata) #8A8479 ~oklch(.585 .012 75).
NEUTRALS (warm concrete): --pb-paper (mortar bg) #E7E3DA ~oklch(.918 .010 78); --pb-surface (bone card) #FFFFFF oklch(1 0 0); --pb-stone (recessed/zebra) #D9D4CA ~oklch(.866 .010 78); --pb-recessed (derivation-drawer well, deeper than stone) #CFC9BE ~oklch(.828 .011 78); --pb-gravel (inactive bars/fills + within-card hairline) #BDB7AC ~oklch(.760 .011 80).
WARMTH MATERIALS (from B — warmth without softening): --pb-paper-warm (the extra-air gutter behind card GROUPS, half a step lighter than paper so whitespace reads intentional) #EDE9E0 ~oklch(.935 .009 80); --pb-rail (teach-in-place context-rail surface, warmest near-white = "read me, you're safe here") #F2EEE6 ~oklch(.952 .008 82).
BLUE (does the structural + data lifting so red stays rare): --pb-navy (structure/header mass) #0A2A5E ~oklch(.300 .090 260); --pb-blue / federal (data emphasis/links/chart bars/hover stroke) #2C6FBF ~oklch(.560 .130 256); --pb-sky (secondary bars/accents) #6FA8DC ~oklch(.715 .090 250); --pb-sky-tint (highlight fill behind the ONE secondary featured metric) #DCE8F6 ~oklch(.925 .025 250).
RED (SIGNAL ONLY — distress score, primary CTA, active parcel): --pb-red #E81828 ~oklch(.585 .225 26) [Phillies true red].
BRICK (TEXT/EDGE ACCENT ONLY — never a fill): --pb-brick #A8341F ~oklch(.485 .150 32) [muddy — wordmark BRICKS, sheriff/aged history, drop-cap, section rule, history-tag pill border].
LENS HUES (held at matched lightness L~.56-.62, lower chroma so fills read as solid blocks not glow; one lens active at a time so color = one meaning): --pb-lens-price (Price & value) #2C6FBF federal family ~oklch(.560 .120 256); --pb-lens-momentum (Development momentum) #3E7D5A weathered park-green ~oklch(.560 .085 158); --pb-lens-distress (Distress & risk) #E81828 the Phillies-red ramp ~oklch(.585 .225 26) [red=distress, aligns both systems, the one lens allowed signal-red as fill]; --pb-lens-livability (Livability crime+311) #B5703A desaturated terracotta / the ONE warm lens, deliberately distinct from both reds ~oklch(.625 .100 58).
FUNCTIONAL: focus ring #2C6FBF 3px solid offset 2px; link #2C6FBF underline; selection bg sky-tint / text ink. AA (light): ink-on-mortar 12.8:1, ink-on-bone 16.1:1, navy-on-bone 11.9:1, white-on-red ~4.6:1 (red text only at >=15px bold OR white-on-red fill — never red body), white-on-navy >7:1, brick-on-bone 6.4:1 (AA all sizes — safe for small aged-history accents).
darkTokens:
Dark = extend the BRAND.md Draft-Navy blueprint/concrete world into a WARM-UMBER lamplit instrument — "the survey table under a desk lamp." NOT blue-black, NOT neon, NOT glass. Per the brief's "warm-dark," the ground leans umber (B) while the whole UI still moves onto the blueprint family (A's rigor), so dark mode is coherent with the always-blueprint map.
GROUNDS: --pb-paper (bg) #1B1814 ~oklch(.215 .009 60) (night-version of mortar, warm umber-black); --pb-surface (card) #25211B ~oklch(.275 .010 62) (lamplit, warm not blue); --pb-stone (zebra/secondary) #2F2A22 ~oklch(.320 .011 64); --pb-recessed (derivation well) #161310 ~oklch(.185 .008 58); --pb-rail (context rail, warmest dark = keeps the "invitation" after dark) #2A251D ~oklch(.295 .011 66); --pb-paper-warm (group gutter) #211D17 ~oklch(.250 .010 62).
BORDERS + TEXT (invert to warm bone): hard structural border --pb-ink renders as near-black #0C1016 ~oklch(.150 .010 250) so the 3px party-wall + offset shadow still read as mass; primary text --pb-text #ECE6DA ~oklch(.918 .010 82) (warm bone, NOT cold white, 13.8:1 on bg); within-card hairline --pb-gravel #3A4555 ~oklch(.380 .016 252); muted --pb-slate #A39A8B ~oklch(.705 .015 70) (5.2:1, AA on surface).
BLUE (lifted for AA): --pb-navy (header mass, lifted to read as a block) #16407F ~oklch(.380 .105 260); --pb-blue / federal #5C97D8 ~oklch(.665 .115 252) (5.4:1); --pb-sky #8FBCE6 ~oklch(.780 .080 248); --pb-sky-tint (featured-metric fill on dark, a muted navy) #25344A ~oklch(.330 .035 250).
RED (holds meaning, lifted ~6% L for AA): --pb-red #FF3A47 ~oklch(.650 .218 26) (~4.9:1 on bg, still unmistakably Phillies). BRICK (aged text on dark): --pb-brick #D9663F ~oklch(.640 .135 38) (6.0:1).
BLUEPRINT (native habitat in dark): --pb-draft-bg #1C2530, --pb-draft-line (survey lines, map + decorative grid) #7FB0E0 ~oklch(.730 .085 250).
LENS HUES lifted for AA, matched-lightness preserved (L~.66): price #5C97D8 / momentum #5FA77E / distress #FF3A47 / livability #D69A4E.
FUNCTIONAL: focus ring #5C97D8 3px offset 2px. Offset shadows in dark use #0C1016 (the darker border ink) so hard-shadow mass still reads. prefers-reduced-motion respected in BOTH themes (resolution/lens transitions become instant swaps; offset shadows stay — they are structure, not motion). AA verified: bone-on-bg 13.8:1, white-on-red(#FF3A47) ~4.9:1, text-on-navy-block >7:1.
structure:
Brutalist constants, non-negotiable, with A's mass tiers and B's air.
BORDERS: default party-wall = 3px solid --pb-ink (--pb-border-w). PROMOTE primary structural divisions (page header band, table outer frame, hero block, lens-switcher rail, context rail) to 4px (--pb-border-w-lg) for extra mass. Within-card hairline = 1px solid --pb-gravel ONLY (ledger row dividers, sub-metric splits). Borders are FULL on all four sides — a >1px border-LEFT-only colored accent stripe is BANNED (the full 3px border is the language; a side-stripe is not).
RADIUS: 0 EVERYWHERE, no exceptions, not even avatars ("cut-brick" corners). --pb-radius:0.
OFFSET HARD SHADOWS (the sanctioned brutalist exception — no blur, ever; soft/blurred shadows BANNED): --pb-shadow 6px 6px 0 var(--pb-ink) on cards/marks/raised pills; --pb-shadow-lg 8px 8px 0 on the hero block and primary CTA; --pb-shadow-mass 10px 10px 0 reserved for the SINGLE page-defining block per screen (e.g. the distress-score hero). Offset always down-right (+x,+y), implying one top-left raking light, consistent across the whole instrument. Shadows never stack on shadows. PRESSED/active state = translate(2px,2px) + shadow shrinks to 4px 4px (the block "sits down") — no scale-bounce.
VISIBLE GRID: a 12-col grid with 3px --pb-ink primary dividers shown at section breaks; metric strips are equal-width cells divided by 3px rules (not gaps); 1px --pb-gravel dividers within a card.
SPACING (B's loosened rhythm so blocks breathe): scale 4/8/12/14/18/22/34/48 (--pb-space-1..--pb-space-9). Group gutters between cards = 34px (so the 6px shadows read and don't collide); card internal padding = 22px; NEW --pb-space-9 48px for air between major page sections; card groups sit on --pb-paper-warm so whitespace is a deliberate material, not emptiness. Data tables stay dense: ledger rows 32-36px so a deep-dive reads like a printout page.
LAYOUT: app shell = fixed Navy top band (4px bottom border) + left structural rail on desktop; content on Mortar; right-side context rail (4px border, --pb-rail surface) holds teach-in-place/glossary/source links — never a floating modal. Cards never touch (min 34px gutter). MOBILE: left rail collapses to a bottom-pinned lens-switcher bar (still 3px-bordered blocks); shadows reduce to 4px to save width; 12-col grid stacks to one column of full-bleed bordered blocks; context rail becomes an in-flow expandable block beneath its trigger.
componentLanguage:
Everything is built from one hard-border kit — the proof that structure does not equal opacity.
CARDS: Bone --pb-surface, 3px ink border, 6px offset shadow, square, on --pb-paper-warm gutters; header = Tanker or Satoshi-900 small-caps over a 3px divider.
METRIC BLOCK: Space Mono tracked uppercase label (xs11, slate) over Space Mono-700 number; cells in an equal-width strip divided by 3px rules. The ONE most-important metric per card flips to solid --pb-red fill + white Space Mono (the only red allowed as a fill — this is the red-budget enforcer; exactly one red metric per card). A secondary featured metric may use --pb-sky-tint fill (NOT red).
LEDGER ROWS (the table = receipts surface, set as survey printout): zebra via --pb-stone on alt rows (tint, not shadow), Satoshi small-caps 700 header row on Navy/ink fill, 1px gravel row dividers, Satoshi label-left / Space Mono tabular value right-aligned into a column. EVERY value-bearing cell carries a dotted-underline (1px dotted --pb-slate, underline-offset 3px) = click-to-source, with a trailing SOURCE STAMP.
SOURCE STAMP: inline Space Mono xs, slate, format [OPA · 2026-06-12] / [L&I '23] / [RTT] / [SHERIFF '98]; click expands the record link in the context RAIL (no modal); hover/focus reveals "Where this comes from" + freshness; a quiet "Where this comes from · refreshed Xd ago" line sits under each data section in Zodiak-italic-small.
VALUE-DERIVATION DRAWER: a recessed (--pb-recessed) well opening below the estimate, 3px top border; collapsed shows "$268k" dotted-underlined; expanded shows plain-English Zodiak "3 arms-length comps within 0.3mi · median $/SF 235 × 1,140 SF · −4% condition = $268k" with each operand dotted-underlined to its comp/source. Expand/collapse = height transition (reduced-motion = instant).
DECOMPOSABLE DISTRESS BAR: horizontal stacked bar, 3px ink border, radius 0, segments butted with 1px ink seams; each segment = a distress component (Tax-delinquent, Vacant, Sheriff sale, L&I violations) sized by contribution; the dominant/alerting segment in true --pb-red, lesser segments in a desaturated brick→gravel ramp; hovering any segment surfaces {component, raw_value, normalized 0-1, weight, contribution, source_url} in the rail. The score readout above it is the screen's one red metric block.
BUTTONS: square, 3px border. PRIMARY = red fill + white Space Mono bold + "→", 8px offset shadow (the screen's CTA = the red budget). SECONDARY = ink fill + Mortar/bone Satoshi label + "+". TERTIARY/ghost = transparent + 3px ink border + ink label.
PILLS/TAGS: Space Mono 10px bold, 2px ink border, square. danger = red fill/white (TAX-DELINQUENT, VACANT — counts against red budget); neutral = ink fill/bone; aged = bone fill + --pb-brick text + brick border (SHERIFF '98 — brick is text/edge only).
CHARTS: blocky bars, 2px ink borders, no gridlines (except blueprint); bars Federal/Sky, the one highlighted/alerting bar red or (median) brick.
LENS SWITCHER: inline segmented control of 4 hard-bordered cells (3px); active cell = ink fill + bone label + a 3px red bottom-marker BLOCK (a chunk, not a thin stripe) OR active-cell fill in that lens's hue at block strength; only one active so color means one thing.
CONTEXT RAIL: right-side 4px-bordered --pb-rail column holding glossary definitions (Zodiak, generous leading), source links, freshness — never a floating modal. GLOSSARY TERM: dotted-underline inline; click expands a one-sentence Zodiak definition into the rail.
EMPTY STATES: the voice in Zodiak ("No comps within a quarter mile. Widen the radius or check the next block over.").
mapTreatment:
Map adopts BRAND.md BLUEPRINT mode as its PERMANENT technical skin (same in light AND dark — the map is always the survey table, and is the night habitat of the whole dark palette) and is the front-door hero; chrome recedes as hard blocks, never floating glass.
GROUND: --pb-draft-bg #1C2530; survey lines / street grid / parcel & neighborhood outlines in --pb-draft-line #7FB0E0 as 1px hairlines; sparse Space Mono labels until zoom.
MULTI-RESOLUTION: city/ZIP → neighborhood → tract → parcel; transitions by opacity/scale ease-out (reduced-motion = instant swap). City default foregrounds neighborhood NAMES as the unit (Passyunk ≠ Point Breeze).
4-LENS CHOROPLETH on the blueprint ground, ONE lens active at a time, sequential ramp from that lens's single hue, all held at MATCHED lightness so the legend never has one lens screaming: Price = federal-blue ramp; Momentum = park-green ramp; Distress = Phillies-RED ramp (red=distress aligns both systems and is the one lens allowed signal-red as fill — the lens IS the alert); Livability = terracotta #B5703A ramp (the one warm tone, distinct from both reds). Fills render as flat blocks at ~70% opacity so survey lines read through, 1px draft-line seams between polygons (the visible grid), no glow/blur.
ACTIVE PARCEL = single --pb-red highlight regardless of active lens: 3px red outline + red surveyor corner-registration ticks at the four corners (grafted from Survey & Record), no glow — the one persistent red on the map, part of the per-screen budget. Hover = 1px federal-blue stroke (not red, so red stays the active-only signal).
LEGEND: bottom-right card on --pb-rail with breathing padding (not crammed onto map chrome), plain-language Zodiak caption ("Darker = more tax-delinquent on this block") + Space Mono numeric break ticks (min · median · max + units) + the active lens hue as square chips.
LENS SWITCHER: bottom-left 3px-bordered segmented block. INSTRUMENT READOUT (quiet, from A): top-right Space Mono cursor coords + scale bar on the blueprint ground, like a survey plot header. TIME CONTROL: bordered slider strip with "tracking since {date}" in Space Mono for state-derived metrics.
TECH: MapLibre + PMTiles base; deck.gl only for advanced overlays (per CONCEPT_v2).
transparencyAndVoice:
All Rowhouse mechanics survive intact, rebuilt in brutalist hardware.
SOURCE STAMPS: every figure (assessment, sale, estimate, distress score, comp) renders dotted-underlined with a trailing Space Mono source stamp [OPA·date]/[L&I]/[RTT]/[SHERIFF '98]; the parcel page AND the API expose the same shape per distress component {component, raw_value, normalized 0-1, weight, contribution, source_url} so screen and data agree.
VALUE DERIVATION: expandable plain-English Zodiak drawer with every operand dotted-underlined to its comp/record — never a black box ("numbers don't lie, people do — here's the receipt").
TEACH-IN-PLACE: dotted-underline glossary terms expand a one-sentence Zodiak definition into the context RAIL (warm --pb-rail surface, generous leading, reads like a margin note from someone who lives there) — never a modal; a quiet "Where this comes from · refreshed Xd ago" freshness line under every data section.
MULTI-RES 4-LENS SCAN is the front door (city→neighborhood→tract→parcel) with numeric break ticks + plain caption + "tracking since" time control.
COMMUNITY-VALUE FRAMING (woven in, not bolted): vacant/distressed parcels surface a quiet "Community signal" Zodiak line ("Vacant 3+ yrs — rehab adds a home back to Fishtown"); the Development lens shows net homes-returned + active permits; aggregate strips ("142 vacant · 38 in active rehab this year") frame the user as a participant in recovery — set in park-green, never red, no confetti.
COMPS: arms-length only, min-sample N≥5 widening ladder, p5/p95 outlier trim, a "why this comp" annotation on each.
VOICE = third-gen South Philadelphian throughout, carried mostly in Zodiak and given more square footage (explainers, empty states, teach rail) so the instrument feels like a neighbor walking you through the file, not a dashboard lecturing you: rooted/specific (blocks & corners, Passyunk ≠ Point Breeze), plain & unimpressed (no hype, no "unlock"/"supercharge", no exclamation points), protective (homes are homes — zero vulture energy), receipts-forward ("numbers don't lie, people do — here's the record"). Headlines in Tanker ("Know the block before you knock."); plain distress labels (TAX-DELINQUENT / VACANT / SHERIFF '98) let the record speak.
WARMTH BUDGET: warmth lives in three places ONLY — the Mortar/paper-warm ground + the warm --pb-rail teach surface, the Zodiak serif voice getting room, and small brick text accents. NEVER in soft corners, blur, tint-elevation, or gradients.
redDiscipline:
Two reds, strict separation, hard cap.
TRUE Phillies Red --pb-red #E81828 (dark #FF3A47) = SIGNAL ONLY, four sanctioned jobs and no others: (1) the distress score / the dominant segment of the decomposable distress bar AND the Distress-lens choropleth (the sanctioned "red=distress" fill, because the segment/lens literally IS the alert); (2) the single primary CTA per screen; (3) the active-parcel highlight + its corner-registration ticks on the map. Danger pills (TAX-DELINQUENT, VACANT) may use it but COUNT against the budget.
MUDDY Brick --pb-brick #A8341F (dark #D9663F) = TEXT/EDGE ACCENT ONLY, never a fill: wordmark "BRICKS", aged/sheriff-history lines (SHERIFF '98, last-sale-1998 deltas), small "why this comp" flags, and the warmth-balanced editorial touches (a drop-cap initial, the rule under a section headline, history-tag pill borders) — always text or 1-2px edge, never a panel fill, never on the same word as true red.
BUDGET: no more than 1-2 true-red elements visible per screen. CONCRETE ACCOUNTING on a deep-dive: distress hero block (red metric fill) = 1; primary "Save this lead →" CTA (red fill) = 2 → BUDGET SPENT — every other action drops to ink/ghost buttons, every other metric uses sky-tint or stays neutral, and danger pills downshift to brick-border/bone-fill so red never becomes wallpaper. ON THE MAP: the active-parcel red = the screen's 1; if the Distress lens is active, the lens ramp IS the red (counts as the encoding, not extra chrome), so the active parcel is the only ADDED red and CTAs on that screen go ink.
The Livability terracotta #B5703A is its own desaturated warm hue, deliberately distinct from both reds so the eye never mistakes "pleasant to live here" for "this is on fire." Blue (Navy structure + Federal data) does ALL the structural and data lifting precisely so red stays rare and therefore LOUD when it appears.
NEVER: red as a card background, red section bands, red body text, red as decoration, two red CTAs, red + brick on the same word. If a screen wants to feel loud, it gets a bigger Tanker headline and a heavier Navy block — not more red.
